  • Develop and improve IT policies, SOPs, and operational procedures.
  • Ensure timely resolution of infrastructure, tooling, and user issues.
  • Coordinate with vendors, ISPs, SaaS/tooling providers, and hardware suppliers.

Collaboration & Enterprise Tools

  • Drive automation and process improvements across IT operations and developer workflows.
  • Participate in technology planning, budgeting, and digital transformation initiatives.

Required Technical Skills

  • Network and system monitoring tools

Success Measures

  • Protect the organization against cybersecurity threats through practical measures.
  • Ensure ongoing IT compliance (licensing, security, regulatory).
  • Improve IT operational efficiency via automation and modern tooling.
  • Build and lead a high‑performing IT team supporting organizational growth.

Brief Description

The Senior Data Coding Specialist serves as a resource for the Data Management (DM) organization and/or project team regarding the coding of clinical trials data (adverse events, medical history, physical examination findings, medications) and coding dictionary issues. Responsible for coding and review of codes applied to clinical trial data, development of SOPs, Data Management Plans, and clinical database validation tools. Interacts with Sponsors to clarify coding guidelines and verify licenses.

Responsibilities

  • Accurately code clinical trial data within project timelines and guidelines, ensuring completeness, accuracy, and consistency.
  • Develop and validate coding specifications, ad‑hoc listings, reports, and queries for data validation.
  • Perform study‑specific User Acceptance Testing (UAT) of the coding module.
  • Conduct peer reviews of coding reports and guide other Data Coding Specialists.
  • Coordinate versioning of assigned dictionaries throughout the study lifecycle.
  • Review or contribute to SOPs, Data Management Plans, and project management tools.
  • Maintain up‑to‑date knowledge of coding dictionaries and guidelines.
  • Interface with project teams to resolve coding‑related issues.
  • Train Data Coding Specialists and DM staff on dictionary structures, conventions, and coding software.
  • Interact with Sponsors to understand coding specifications, receive feedback, and promote consistency.
  • Verify active sponsor dictionary subscriptions.

Qualification

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in a related discipline with 4 years of experience, or HS diploma with 7 years of experience.
  • Experience: 2‑4 years of medical coding experience in pharmaceutical and/or contract research organization.

Required Skills

  • Strong knowledge of medical terminology and coding dictionaries.
  • Understanding of clinical trials concepts.
  • Proficiency with EDC coding applications (preferably Medidata Rave).
  • Proficient with Microsoft Office Suite.
  • Excellent written, oral, and presentation communication skills.
  • Strong organizational, problem‑solving, and analytical abilities.
  • Ability to manage priorities, workflow, and multiple projects.
  • Versatility, flexibility, and adaptability to changing priorities.
  • Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work with diverse individuals.
  • Commitment to excellence, creativity, and teamwork.
  • Ability to work independently and within committees.
  • Good judgement for timely decision‑making.

Preferred Skills

  • Additional knowledge of clinical trials concepts.
  • Further experience with EDC coding applications (Medidata Rave preferred).
  • Extensive medical coding experience in pharma or CRO settings.
summary: Senior Data Coding Specialist leads coding of clinical trial data, ensuring accuracy and compliance. Requires 2-4 years medical coding experience with EDC tools.

The role merges DataOps rigor with a product‑centric mindset, ensuring data is discoverable, governed, and optimized for Generative AI and Agentic AI workflows. Responsibilities include production readiness, governance, master data management, and end‑to‑end lifecycle ownership from ingestion to secure consumption via Unity Catalog.

Key Responsibilities

Data Governance & Master Data Management (MDM)

  • Lead technical implementation of MDM solutions to create a “Golden Record” for clinical trials.
  • Enforce data governance policies via automated “policy‑as‑code” throughout the data product lifecycle.
  • Utilize Databricks Unity Catalog for centralized access control, auditing, and data lineage across the Azure tenant.
  • Collaborate with data stewards to automate identification and remediation of sensitive data (PII/PHI) in the data lakehouse.

Data Product Engineering & AI Readiness

  • Define and maintain SLAs, SLOs, and SLIs for data quality and availability.
  • Engineer API‑based integrations for consumption by internal applications, LLMs, and external partners.
  • Ensure high‑quality metadata is included in data products for Generative AI applications.

DataOps Mastery (Data Kitchen & IBM Best Practices)

  • Architect the “Data Kitchen Innovation Pipeline” for rapid, automated deployment of new data features.
  • Implement IBM DataOps best practices to create a unified data virtualization and governance layer.
  • Automate the “Value Pipeline” using CI/CD for continuous monitoring of data quality and statistical drift.
  • Foster an observability culture with automated testing in the data product (governance, MDM) pipeline.

Technical Requirements

  • Governance & MDM: Proven experience building MDM frameworks and automated data governance tools in regulated environments.
  • Cloud Infrastructure: Advanced expertise in Azure, including Azure Data Factory, Azure DevOps, and Azure Key Vault.
  • Databricks Stack: Mastery of Databricks, especially Unity Catalog and Delta Lake.
  • Modern Integration: Strong proficiency in PySpark, SQL, RESTful APIs, and workflow scheduling with Apache Airflow, Databricks Lakeflow, or Prefect.

Preferred Skills

(Repeated from Technical Requirements)

Why Worldwide Clinical Trials?

You will shape the data strategy, revolutionizing how clinical data drives life‑saving treatments. We value automation, transparency, and quality as we build the “Trusted Factory” for tomorrow’s clinical trials.
summary: Lead data product engineering for clinical trials, driving MDM, governance, and AI readiness on Azure and Databricks. Ensure scalable, compliant data products.

Job Summary

We are looking for a Senior Automation Test Engineer to design and implement testing practices that combine automation frameworks with AI‑powered testing tools, and to roll them out directly with engineering teams. The role covers hands‑on automation development, along with defining test strategies, standards, and workflows that keep AI‑assisted testing consistent and effective across projects. The role includes on‑site work with project and client teams as a regular part of the job, in addition to remote work. Beyond building automation, the position involves understanding why a testing practice may not be landing with a particular team and adjusting the approach – which requires reading organizational context, not just technical systems.

Key Responsibilities

Design the Testing Practice

  • Define test strategy templates, automation standards, and AI‑usage guidelines for testers and engineers.
  • Evaluate AI‑powered testing tools and frameworks; recommend adoption per project/team.
  • Determine where AI tools are used in the workflow (test generation, automation, defect analysis, reporting) and where manual review is required.

Build & Implement Automation

  • Design, build, and maintain automation frameworks and scripts for web, mobile, API, and integration testing.
  • Integrate automated testing into CI/CD pipelines, including automated quality gates and release validation.
  • Roll out testing practices directly with engineering teams and, when needed, at client locations – supporting adoption on the ground.

Improve Through Feedback

  • Identify why a testing approach isn’t adopted and adjust based on root cause.
  • Capture successes and failures from each rollout and feed back into shared standards and frameworks.
  • Coordinate with development and deployment teams to ensure testing connects smoothly with the delivery pipeline.

Required Skills

Automation & Quality Engineering

  • 3–6 years hands‑on automation testing and quality engineering across web, mobile, API, and integration.
  • Strong experience with automation frameworks such as Playwright, Selenium, Cypress, Appium, or similar.
  • Experience designing, building, and maintaining scalable automation frameworks.
  • Strong understanding of testing methodologies, test design techniques, and QA best practices.
  • Experience integrating automated testing into CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Azure DevOps).
  • Knowledge of release validation, deployment testing, smoke testing, and quality gates.
  • Proficiency with Git and collaborative development workflows.

AI Tools & Usage

  • Daily experience using AI‑powered testing tools for test generation, automation development, defect analysis, and reporting.
  • Working knowledge of multiple AI tools/models and ability to select appropriate ones.
  • Experience evaluating emerging AI testing tools and recommending adoption.
  • Ability to write clear prompts and correct AI mistakes.
  • Active interest in new AI tools, models, and techniques.

Communication

  • Strong written and spoken English.
  • Ability to interpret organizational context and explain testing strategy to technical and non‑technical stakeholders, including client‑facing settings.

Preferred Skills

  • Experience rolling out new testing practices/frameworks across multiple teams or clients.
  • Willingness to travel for on‑site implementation.
  • Familiarity with defect tracking and test management tools.
  • Experience mentoring QA engineers.
  • Understanding of development and deployment pipeline structures.

Educational Qualification

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or equivalent practical industry experience.

Salary Package

10 – 18 LPA
summary: Senior Automation Test Engineer leading AI‑assisted testing practice, designing frameworks and guiding teams. Requires 3‑6 years automation experience with AI tools.
